Browser YATU over a throwaway worktree backend (fixture: real clip blob + synthetic 3-step timeline). Live DOM+API reads all green: video 640x480 readyState4; ruler rendered 3 timeline steps; clicking step2 (tMs1200) SEEKED video.currentTime→1.2 (byte-range); ⏱ inserted '▶0:01 · select eval' (time + ≤T step); drag-circle POSTed the paused frame to /api/yatsu/blob and prefilled an anchored comment ('▶0:01 · select eval' + ![frame](/api/yatsu/blob/<hash>)); sending it lazily CREATED the eval's local Issue 'eval: video-evidence · video-plays-in-eval-tab' whose body leads with the anchor, carries the frame link, AND indexes the hash on thread.evidence[]; the sent comment shows an anchor chip that SEEKED the clip on click and renders the circled frame inline; the fail verdict filed a manual@1 reading with blob=null (NO marks transcript). @new-with-anchor not re-spawned here (avoids a stray worker) — covered by construction (comment leads with the anchor; newWorkerPrompt embeds the body verbatim) + the eval-comments dispatch measurement. ⏱ glyph shows as tofu in headless (emoji-font gap), renders for real users.
